title: Matching Unique Identifiers and Summarising Matched Data in a Different Board
excerpt:
We work with volunteers that log their hours via a form. 
How might we calculate the hours of each volunteer so that it shows a running total of each in another board? 
EG: Tony works 3 shifts of 4 hours. 
In another boa…

posts.author: mkoh (Ming-En Koh)
posts.cookedHtml:
We work with volunteers that log their hours via a form.

How might we calculate the hours of each volunteer so that it shows a running total of each in another board?

EG: Tony works 3 shifts of 4 hours.

In another board, under Tony’s name, it shows 12 hours.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Use a connected board column. Ie Board 1 is the volunteers, Board 2 is the forms. Each form is connected to a volunteer. Then on Board 1 you do a mirror column to pull in the a column that includes the hours (it will total it)

posts.author: mkoh (Ming-En Koh)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Clover_Gal!

Thanks for the response.

When you say “Each Form”, do you mean each form response ie Item?

I’m having trouble visualising how that works.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Correct, each form response ie item would then be connected to a volunteer on the other board.
